title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Miss Bunner, the housekeeper, confides her suspicions about Patrick, a relative of Miss Blacklock, to Miss Marple. Miss Bunner expresses gratitude for the kindness shown by Lotte and mentions that Miss Blacklock promised her a pension and furniture in her will. However, Miss Bunner believes Miss Blacklock is too trusting and notes that Patrick has twice feigned debt to ask her for money. Miss Bunner suspects Patrick may be morally responsible for the suicide of a Swiss young man and believes he may have been involved in the recent "terrible business."

Miss Bunner shares two specific observations: she once caught Patrick in the shrubbery holding a greasy cup, and she overheard him arguing with Julia. During that argument, Patrick claimed he was warning Julia against a black market deal, though Miss Bunner expresses doubt regarding his sincerity. Additionally, Miss Bunner suspects Patrick may have tampered with a table lamp in the drawing room. Their conversation is interrupted by Miss Blacklock, prompting Miss Bunner to hastily change the subject.

After Miss Blacklock and Miss Bunner depart, Round asks what was discussed. Miss Marple reflects on the power of family ties, citing a case where a daughter protected her father from a poisoning charge. Miss Marple then engages Round in a conversation about a former parlour maid named Miss Florence, noting that the maid was easily attracted to polite men.
